Organic Chemistry I Lectures CHEM-C 341, Fall 2020
Week 13 Lecture Slides – Week of 11/16/2020
Instructor: Kasey J. Clear

Molecules can take on many different transient forms
Conformation = spatial arrangement between groups due to rotation around a single ( σ ) bond (usually between two carbon atoms)
Conformer = specific conformation
Conformations vs. Configurations
Barriers to rotation around different types of bonds

Ethane Conformations
Newman Projection = representation of the view down a C-C bond
Potential energy vs . angle for ethane:
Why is eclipsed less stabl e than staggered conformation?

Propane Conformations
*Eclipsed is 1 3 kcal/ mol higher in energy than staggered

Butane Conformations
Butane (and longer chains) have more interesting conformers:
Steric strain = additional strain experienced in a molecule when atoms are close together

Questions and practice
Does it make sense to draw the structural diagrams as zig-zags like we do ? Are we more likely to find the chains in this conformation?
Practice
Draw the three staggere d conformers of 3 -methylpentane around the C2-C3 bond as a Newman projection and comment on their relative stability

Adolph von Baeyer
White Indigo (water soluble)
-Developed a synthesis of indigo
-Discovered phenolphthalein and fluorescein dyes
-Contributed to understanding of ring strain in cycloalkanes
Oxidation

Cycloalkane Conformations
Causes of strain (instability) in rings:
1) Bond angle strain (Baeyer strain) – sp 3 carbon wants to be 109.5°
Cyclopropane (triangle)
Cyclobutane (square)
Cyclopentane (pentagon)
Cyclohexane (hexagon )
2) Torsional strain – eclipsing interactions are unavoidable in some rings
Cycloalkane Conformations

Cycloalkane Conformations
Causes of strain (instability) in rings:
1) Bond angle strain (Baeyer strain) – sp 3 carbon wants to be 109.5°
2) Torsional strain – eclipsing interactions cause more instability

Cycloalkane Conformations
Cyclohexane is strain-free in its chair conformation!
1) Bond angles can be 109.5°
2) All bonds can be fully staggered

Chair cyclohexane has two types of hydrogens: axial and equatorial
Why do we care?
β -glucose
How to draw chair cyclohexane
Interconversion between chair conformations

Interconversion of methylcyclohexane
Chair 1 is 7.3 kJ/ mol higher in energy than Chair 2
Newman projection:
1,3-diaxial interaction : steric strain between an axial substituent and other axial groups on the same face of the ring

Substituent bulkiness
Sterically larger group has greater preference for the equatorial position

Multiple substituents – which chair is favored?
Things to consider:
-Relative position on the ring
-Relative stereochemistry
-Relative size of substituents
In general…want more groups equatorial and larger groups equatorial
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
Draw both chair conformers and indicate which is favored :
Note: placement of groups on chair projection is arbitrary
1,4 and 1,3-disubstituted cyclohexane
Many d ifferent substituents: how to decide

Multiple substituents – “stuck” ring systems
t ert -butyl is too bulky to occupy the axial position

Bicyclic molecules
General types of bicyclic molecules:
Fused (e.g. decalin and cholesterol)
Bridged ( bornanone /camphor)
Spiro (e.g. spiropyran )
"However, the assembly of spiropyran requires mind-numbing steps, limiting their synthetic accessibility.“ ( Macromol . Rapid Commun . 2012, 33, 1958)

Substitution reactions on substituted cyclohexane
S N 2 substitution is faster when the leaving group is axial

Substitution reactions on substituted cyclohexane
S N 2 substitution is faster when the leaving group is axial
Practice

Practice
Here is a Newman projection of a disubstituted cyclohexane ring.
Draw the chair structure of this conformer and draw the structure of the other chair conformer.
Which of the conformers is more stable?

Draw the equivalent structure using wedges and dashes on a planar cyclohexane (hexagon).
Provide a systematic name for the molecule (include cis or trans at the start of the name). Bromine as a substituent = bromo
Would you expect more of the cis or trans product upon reaction with sodium cyanide?
